summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch must handle minor Claude JSON key drift and fail closed with explicit blockers on invalid agent JSON.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Minor recoverable JSON drift is recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Minor recoverable JSON drift is recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
risks: Malformed provider output could be silently accepted or produce unclear blockers that hide readiness failure.

verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements JSON drift repair and fail-closed invalid JSON handling as specified in the demand acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: JSON repair logic in parseJsonCandidate function handles Claude drift: line ranges, escaped backslashes, spacing around keys, trailing commas
evidence.why: This is the core implementation of minor recoverable JSON drift repair.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject function attempts multiple parse strategies: direct parse, fenced code blocks, sliced object extraction, with error tracking
evidence.why: Shows multi-strategy JSON extraction with repair attempts and explicit error reporting.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when parsed is null, with blocked verdict and raw output excerpt
evidence.why: This is the fail-closed behavior for unrecoverable invalid JSON.

evidence.summary: Provider run JSON extraction with json_repaired flag tracking, passing parseError to normalization, and truncating output
evidence.why: Shows the runtime integration that records repair status and propagates parse errors.

evidence.summary: Test repairs minor Claude JSON key drift including: line 42-88 range format, escaped dot, double-space around key, markdown code block wrapper
evidence.why: Test evidence that confirms drift repair works for real Claude output patterns.

evidence.summary: Test verifies invalid agent JSON returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON blocker code on all agent results and readiness blockers
evidence.why: Test evidence that confirms fail-closed behavior with explicit blocker code.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot uses git ls-files for tracked files and walkFiles for full tree, with entry digest via hash/stat
evidence.why: Evidence of boundary detection implementation using Git-backed file tracking.

evidence.summary: Runtime creates boundary snapshot before agent loop, creates another after loop, diffs snapshots, and records violations
evidence.why: Evidence of complete boundary audit workflow around agent execution.

evidence.summary: Test verifies boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, with violated status and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker
evidence.why: Test evidence that confirms boundary enforcement is independent of agent result success.

evidence.summary: Test verifies agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked during normalization with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED
evidence.why: Test evidence that confirms scope validation is enforced at normalization time.

risks: Malformed provider output could be silently accepted if parseJsonCandidate returns a false positive through the repair regexes
JSON repair regexes may not cover all future Claude output drift patterns
Boundary detection using git ls-files may miss untracked files in non-git repos

result.notes: All acceptance criteria verified: minor drift repair is implemented and tested, invalid JSON fails closed with explicit error code, readiness blocking is confirmed, and boundary enforcement is independent of agent success.

agent_results.claim: Explorer evidence is sufficient to verify that YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements minor recoverable JSON drift repair and fail-closed invalid JSON handling per acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate function implements repair regexes for Claude drift: line ranges, escaped backslashes, spacing, trailing commas
evidence.why: Primary evidence for recoverable drift repair implementation.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON with blocked verdict when parsed is null after all extraction strategies
evidence.why: Primary evidence for fail-closed invalid JSON handling.

evidence.summary: Test repairs real Claude drift patterns: line ranges, escaped keys, spacing, code blocks
evidence.why: Test coverage confirming drift repair works for specified patterns.

evidence.summary: Test verifies invalid JSON returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON on agent results and readiness blockers
evidence.why: Test coverage confirming fail-closed blocker propagation.

risks: Repair regexes could theoretically false-positive on malformed but structurally valid JSON
Future Claude output drift patterns may not match current repair regexes

result.notes: Explorer provided 4 primary evidence records directly supporting the acceptance criteria with exact line numbers from code and tests. Boundary evidence is extra but not required. Identified risks are inherent to regex-based repair and already documented in tests. No facts were asserted without evidence. No blockers remain.
